items.title: Heated Rivalry stars Hudson Williams and Francois Arnaud denounce hateful comments
items.summary_auto: Actors Hudson Williams and Francois Arnaud posted matching statements to their Instagram accounts on Monday night, calling out hateful comments made by fans of the hit hockey romance show.

items.title: Canadian Heated Rivalry stars denounce hateful comments
items.summary_auto: Actors Hudson Williams and Francois Arnaud posted matching statements to their Instagram accounts on Monday night, calling out hateful comments made by fans of the hit hockey romance show.

items.title: WNBA, players' union to meet as deadline nears to start season on time: report
items.summary_auto: The WNBA and the players' union are set to meet Tuesday to continue discussions on reaching a new collective bargaining agreement as a deadline to get the season started on time is at hand, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press.

items.title: Hamilton's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander ties NBA 20-point streak record
items.summary_auto: On Monday night, Canada's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander matched Wilt Chamberlain's NBA record of 126 consecutive regular-season games of 20 or more points.

items.title: Montrealer charged in Ryan Wedding-linked murder plot pleads not guilty
items.summary_auto: A Montreal man charged in connection with a murder plot allegedly ordered by accused drug kingpin Ryan Wedding — and said to involve a Quebec hitman known as Tupac — has pleaded not guilty in a U.S. court.

items.title: Ontario family reunited with war medal missing since veteran died in 2011
items.summary_auto: The family of Canadian soldier Stefan Jankowski has been reunited with a military medal that had been missing since the time of his death 2011. He’s from Windsor, Ont., and was a trooper with the Royal Canadian Dragoons, serving in Afghanistan, where he earned the medal.

items.title: First Nations chiefs go to Alberta legislature, pressure province to end separation debate
items.summary_auto: More than a dozen First Nations chiefs and even more band councillors and elders were at Alberta's legislature Monday, calling on Premier Danielle Smith's government to stomp out the push for the province to quit Canada.

items.title: Major freezing rain storm could affect large swath of Eastern Canada, Environment Canada warns
items.summary_auto: Environment Canada is warning of a major freezing rain storm spanning parts of Ontario, Quebec and the Atlantic provinces, which the weather agency says could lead to power outages and dangerous road conditions.

items.title: 'We got it wrong': N.S. government reverses course on some budget cuts after outcry
items.summary_auto: Supports and programming for people with disabilities, seniors and African Nova Scotian and Indigenous students will be maintained, reversing the cuts that were previously announced.

items.title: Canada's Natalie Wilkie skis to 3rd medal of Milano Cortina, earning sprint classic bronze
items.summary_auto: Canadian Para nordic skiing star Natalie Wilkie has now won a medal of every colour at the Milano-Cortina Paralympics, adding bronze in the women's standing sprint classic on Tuesday in Val di Fiemme.

items.title: Markets steady, crude oil prices ease on Tuesday after volatile start to week
items.summary_auto: Trading steadied early Tuesday and crude prices eased after wild swings this week with investors trying to figure out how long the war with Iran will continue.

items.title: Strike aftermath leaves many Alberta teachers isolated, looking for new work
items.summary_auto: CBC News heard from 6,000 teachers and other educators across the province with a nuanced view of life in Alberta classrooms. Many worry a staffing crisis is on the horizon and express little hope for substantial change. There was a lot of anger — much of it directed at the government, some at union officials, and in a few cases, against school district leaders.

items.title: Dozens of Haitian civilians killed by police, security company drones in past year, Human Rights Watch says
items.summary_auto: Drones operated by Haitian security forces and private contractors have killed at least 1,243 people and injured 738 others in less than a year, Human Rights Watch said Tuesday.

items.title: Canadian Coast Guard captain fired for 'willfully' ignoring distress call: labour board
Investigators search Epstein's former New Mexico ranch
items.summary_auto: The search follows New Mexico Attorney General ​Raul Torrez's decision last month to reopen the investigation into the late sex offender's alleged criminal activities at the ranch 48 ​kilometres south of the state's capital of Santa Fe.

items.title: Police release video of suspect vehicle fleeing 'national security incident' shooting at U.S. consulate in Toronto
items.summary_auto: Police say they are looking for two male suspects who reportedly stopped outside the consulate in a white vehicle around 4:30 a.m. and fired multiple rounds at the building. Though police have not provided a possible motive, it follows three separate shootings at synagogues in the Greater Toronto Area at a time of war in the Middle East.

items.title: Police call shooting at U.S. consulate in Toronto 'national security incident,' seek 2 suspects
items.summary_auto: Police say they are looking for two male suspects who reportedly stopped outside the consulate in a white vehicle around 4:30 a.m. and fired multiple rounds at the building. Though police have not provided a possible motive, it follows three separate shootings at synagogues in the Greater Toronto Area at a time of war in the Middle East.

items.title: Parents of security guard killed in Edmonton robbery push for parole law changes
items.summary_auto: The parents of an Edmonton security guard killed in an armed robbery in 2012 are pushing for legislative changes that would limit parole eligibility for their son’s killer and other convicted murderers in Canada.

items.title: Manitobans denied assisted dying at 5 times national average, federal report shows
items.summary_auto: A Steinbach woman and her family don't understand why she doesn't qualify for MAID in a province that denies access far more than anywhere else in Canada.

items.title: Notorious N.B. serial killer Allan Legere dead at 78
items.summary_auto: Miramichi serial killer Allan Legere, who terrorized the Miramichi for seven months in 1989, has died at the age of 78.

items.title: The boat was named The Getaway, but the thief didn't
items.summary_auto: The Toronto man who drove from Alberta to the small coastal community of D'Escousse, N.S., where he stole a boat last fall, told court officials he was on the run from organized crime and was headed for Morocco to seek asylum.

items.title: Never smoked before? You could still be at risk of lung cancer, experts say
items.summary_auto: Research shows a growing number of non- and never-smokers are getting diagnosed with lung cancer. Risks for never-smokers include poor air quality caused by pollution and wildfire smoke, occupational exposures like those for firefighters and construction workers, as well as cancer-causing gases like radon found in an increasing number of homes.

items.title: This Canadian family fuelled Myanmar's civil war with 'blood pearls'
items.summary_auto: A company with Canadian directors funnelled valuable pearls to Myanmar’s brutal military regime long after the 2021 military coup that sparked a bloody civil war — despite sanctions imposed by Canada and its allies. A CBC News investigation has determined the RCMP was asked to look at the activities of Belpearl almost two years ago by a human rights group, but authorities won't say whether they have taken up the case.

items.title: Canada restricts drug boat intel from U.S. navy's Caribbean airstrike operation
items.summary_auto: HMCS Yellowknife has just ended its deployment in a U.S.-led anti-drug-smuggling mission in the Caribbean, where American airstrikes on suspected drug boats have killed over 150 people. Canadian officials say they will ensure that intelligence about boats they detect and track is provided to the U.S. only on condition it not be shared with naval forces involved in lethal airstrikes in the region.

items.title: Is Quebec’s controversial constitution for the province really going to happen?
items.summary_auto: The opposition Liberals – and hundreds of other groups – say the Coalition Avenir Québec’s consultation process on its plan for a Quebec constitution was a failure, and it’s time to scrap the law altogether.

items.title: Why Trump can't afford to brush off the Iran war's economic impact
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump is trying to downplay the effect of the Iran war on the domestic economy even as American drivers are already feeling the impact on their wallets every time they fill up their cars and trucks.

items.title: 3 unidentified orcas spotted in Vancouver Harbour never before documented in B.C.
items.summary_auto: Orcas appear in Vancouver Harbour from time to time and often delight residents who catch a glimpse of them. But marine experts say three whales spotted there last week are unlike any previously recorded in B.C. waters.

items.title: Peter Mansbridge didn't tell Pierre Poilievre, podcast audience about work for South Korean firm
items.summary_auto: Peter Mansbridge says he didn't tell his podcast audience or Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre about work he had done for a South Korean firm vying for Ottawa's defence business during a recent podcast he published, in which he asked the Opposition leader about the issue of Canada's submarine procurement.

items.title: Trump says war in Iran could be over soon, but U.S. hasn't 'won enough' yet
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump hinted on Monday that the war in the Middle East could be over soon — though not this week, he specified — even as hardliners pledged loyalty to their new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei.

items.title: TikTok allowed to keep business in Canada under new rules
items.summary_auto: The federal government said on Monday that it will let TikTok continue to operate in Canada and allow an investment by the tech ‌platform to proceed, after a national security review reversed the conclusion of a previous one.

items.title: FIFA World Cup COO says tournament 'too big' to be postponed amid global turmoil
items.summary_auto: FIFA's World Cup chief operating officer says the tournament is "too big" to be postponed because of global turmoil caused by the U.S. and Israeli war against Iran.

items.title: Non-U.S. trade propels Port of Vancouver to record cargo volumes
items.summary_auto: Freight volumes at the Port of Vancouver rose eight per cent to 170.4 million tonnes in 2025, according to the port authority. The boost was driven by surging exports of grain, crude oil and potash as well as higher container and auto trade, all of which reached record levels.

items.title: Estrogen patch shortage leaves some Albertans in a lurch, looking for alternatives
items.summary_auto: The latest wave of an ongoing shortage of estrogen patches has some Alberta women scrambling to find new menopause treatments.

items.title: Canada commits nearly $1B to drone and airborne defence research
items.summary_auto: The Liberal government has earmarked nearly $1 billion for drone and airborne defence research. The money is going into the National Research Council for a variety of defence initiatives, including the purchase of a Bombardier jet for research.
